calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-20-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Curran for Platte River Insurance Company.
Phillip Campbell in Propria Persona.
Pamala Matson in Propria Persona.
Nick Matson in Propria Persona.

Mr. Campbell advised the Court that the correct amount of the surcharge against Ms. Witherspoon is $23,304.66, stated that the petition is ready to go and that receipts on distribution have not been provided by Nick and Pamala. Mr. Curran stated that they intended to object initially, but have determined to wrap it up and accept the charge. Nick stated that Ms. Witherspoon gave them some money, that she gave him $5,000.00 at a time, that he went through $55,000.00 to $60,000.00, that he called Ms. Witherspoon's attorney (Ms. Ng) and that never got a response from her. Pamala stated that there are some missing numbers apparently for taxes, that the accounting is not acceptable, that a lot of money is missing and that she would like someone to take a look at it. The Amended First, Second and Third and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is taken under submission subject to review of the order and an appropriate surcharge amount towards Ms. Witherspoon. The Order to Show Cause Re: Surcharge is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Phillip Campbell, administrator (by CourtCall); James Curran for Platte River Ins. Co.; Paul Nicholas Matson.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-07-2017 Continued To Mar-21-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Philip Campbell (Administrator) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Campbell stated that he believes that all of the cash assets are there, that tax returns have been filed timely, that there were payments and refunds, that there have been minor technical issues and that he needs to file the 2016 tax returns. Mr. Campbell also stated that he needs to do a petition for final distribution, asked for a continuance stating that one of the heirs wants to investigate into the tangible personal property that went missing when the house was cleaned out, stated that the heir may object to the final accounting and that he will know at that point if there is any surcharge. Furthermore, Mr. Campbell stated that he has been unable to obtain any information from the former Administrator, that there is less than $100.00 interest on penalties, that it could be pulled from the statutory fees and that he is not requesting that the order to appear be taken off calendar. The accounting petition and the order to appear are continued to March 21, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-06-2016 Continued To Feb-07-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Christine Witherspoon. Phillip Campbell in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that the petition for appointment of successor administrator by Mr. Campbell will be granted. Ms. Ng reported that she has not heard from Ms. Witherspoon. The Court stated that an amended accounting will need to be filed and that the accounting will need to be brought up to date from 6/2/16 to 12/6/16. Mr. Campbell stated that they will need to contact the IRS regarding taxes and that he expects this to take 3 months. Ms. Ng stated that she has no access to records and is unable to prepare the accounting. Mr. Campbell stated that they can work together on it. The Court granted the petition for Letters of Administration (subject to review of the order). The accounting petition and the Order to Show Cause Re Removal were continued to February 17, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Continued To Dec-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-28-2016 Continued To Oct-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for (administrator) Christine Witherspoon. Pamela Jean Matson also present.

The Court noted that Ms. Witherspoon is not present. Ms. Ng reported that she sent a text to Ms. Witherspoon this morning to ask if she was coming to court but the response that she received only stated that Ms. Witherspoon had signed the tax papers. The Court stated that it will suspend Ms. Witherspoon as administrator and set a continuance regarding removal and surcharge. Ms. Ng stated that they are at 95% of closing the estate, that they have already filed a petition for final distribution, that she is waiting for amendments from Ms. Witherspoon, that they won't be able to do anything if she is suspended and asked if someone will be appointed in the interim. The Court stated that it first has to deal with the removal and surcharge. Ms. Matson presented a letter regarding her mother's estate to the Court. The Court reviewed the letter and directed Ms. Matson to speak with Ms. Ng regarding her questions. Ms. Matson stated that there is a lot wrong with the accounting, that there were many items that were to be in storage but are not there. The Court instructed Ms. Matson to talk with counsel for clarification and returned the letter to her. The accounting petition was continued to October 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and an Order to Appear will be issued to Ms. Witherspoon.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not Reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Sep-28-2016 Continued To Oct-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-14-2016 Continued To Sep-28-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Christine Witherspoon.

Ms. Ng reported that she sent Ms. Witherspoon an email regarding this hearing, that they were last here on 8/3/16, that Ms. Witherspoon has singe the Power of Attorney for the CPA's, that the decedent had not filed taxes for several years and they need to catch up, that the taxes are just about ready for the final review, that she has not been in communication with Ms. Witherspoon recently and that counsel cannot do anything without the original bank records. The Court stated that this is troubling, that Ms. Witherspoon appeared on 8/3/16 and said this would get done and would be a priority and that the only issue is whether to suspend her today or on the continuance date. Ms. Ng reported that one of the beneficiaries called to say that the storage unit fees had not been paid and Mr. Marchi provided some trust funds that he had been holding. The Court stated that counsel is in a difficult situation and asked if it suspends Ms. Witherspoon is there a beneficiary that could step in. Ms. Ng reported that one of the beneficiaries is under conservatorship and the other two have been homeless since the property was sold. The Court asked if Mr. Marchi would step in. Ms. Ng stated that they have talked about it, that there is a possible conflict of interest, that 1/3 of the trust poured into this estate, that he is the logical choice but is concerned about the ethical issues. The Court asked about the storage fee issue and if it was a monthly fee that had been getting paid. Ms. Ng stated that it must have been but that there is no way for her to track it. The Court asked if it suspends her today how would anything get done. Ms. Ng stated that that is one of the problems, that the accounts are in Ms. Witherspoon's name and that she is not a signatory. The Court continued the accounting petition to September 28, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 stating that it will issue another Order to Appear for Ms. Witherspoon to explain why she is not responding or fulfilling her duties and that the Court will consider suspension or removal on that date. Ms. Ng stated that Mr. Marchi would prefer not to serve due to the difficult beneficiaries but that she will talk to him. The Court asked Ms. Ng to encourage her client to be replaced by someone else.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-03-2016 Continued To Sep-14-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for and with Christine Witherspoon.

The Court stated that Ms. Ng reports that she is having difficulty getting the information to complete the accounting. Ms. Witherspoon stated that she has a backlog of paperwork and that she has reviewed the examiner's notes. Ms. Ng stated that she did not serve additional petitions as she knew that adjustments were needed and that based on a conversation she had with Ms. Witherspoon this morning she will be getting the needed information. The Court addressed Ms. Witherspoon stating that this is not the only case where people are not getting the product/information necessary and asked if she is going to get on top of this. Ms. Witherspoon stated that her active living client list is small, that she had health issues, that a couple of her employees left for various reasons, that it is difficult to get someone trained and that she has two accounting preparers that are ready to go. The Court stated that it is a point of concern of the repeated issues that are arising, that if she cannot keep on top of the work steps will need to be taken to withdraw and that she is creating an expense to the estate and to the Court. The Accounting Petition was continued to September 14, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-23-2016 Continued To Jun-06-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for Christine Witherspoon (Petitioner)

Ms. Ng stated that Christine told her that the accounting will be ready tomorrow and that Christine was working with an accountant to confirm the decedent had filed all of the tax returns. The Court expressed its concerns with the multiple continuances in this cases and that Christine may be surcharged for the entire value of the estate.

Ms. Ng reported that a part of the delay was that the accountant was away, that she has Christine's resignation on hand and that if the Court were to accept her resignation than a successor would need to be appointed right away.

The Court questioned the likelihood of having the petition by June 6,2016. Ms. Ng stated that she has not seen the accounting but believes that June 6, 2016 is fine.

The Court continued the status on close of estate to June 6, 2016 at 9:00a.m., in Dept. 204. If the accounting is not on file, the Court will suspend Christine Witherspoon as of that date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Nancy Wong. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar May-02-2016 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Continued To May-23-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lillian Ng for (administrator) Christine Witherspoon.

The Court stated that it needs a petition for final distribution or a status on administration. Ms. Ng reported that on Friday she received a partial accounting from Ms. Witherspoon, that it is the first time she has received anything and that she would like a 30 day continuance to get the accounting filed. The Court asked if the current bond is sufficient. Ms. Ng stated that there is significant capital gains tax, that she thinks the bond is appropriate at this time (the clerk confirmed that the current bond amount is $400,000.00), that the partial accounting does not show assets on hand, that the sale was for $600,000.00 and that she will need to check with Ms. Witherspoon to see what is on hand. The Court requested that she check and file an ex parte petition to increase bond if necessary. The status on close of estate was continued to May 23, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)
